name: build_object_system
outcome_goal: Build the flexible object system, wire dispatch form, connect modules to objects, seed CRM data

jobs.label: create_object_tables
jobs.prompt:
You are working at the Praxis repository root. Use Postgres via $WORKFLOW_DATABASE_URL (source scripts/_workflow_env.sh && workflow_load_repo_env at repo root). PATH must include /opt/homebrew/bin.

Create and apply migration 017 for the flexible object system.

1. Create file: Code&DBs/Databases/migrations/workflow/017_object_system.sql

Contents:

```sql
-- Migration 017: Flexible Object System
-- Generic object storage that any app template can use

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS object_types (
    type_id text PRIMARY KEY,
    name text NOT NULL,
    description text NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
    icon text,
    property_definitions jsonb NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]',
    created_by text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'system',
    created_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    search_vector tsvector GENERATED ALWAYS AS (
        to_tsvector('english', coalesce(name, '') || ' ' || coalesce(description, ''))
    ) STORED
);

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_object_types_fts ON object_types USING GIN (search_vector);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS objects (
    object_id text PRIMARY KEY,
    type_id text NOT NULL REFERENCES object_types(type_id),
    properties jsonb NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
    status text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active',
    created_by text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'user',
    created_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    updated_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    search_vector tsvector GENERATED ALWAYS AS (
        to_tsvector('english', coalesce(properties::text, ''))
    ) STORED
);

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_objects_type ON objects (type_id);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_objects_status ON objects (type_id, status);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_objects_props ON objects USING GIN (properties);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_objects_fts ON objects USING GIN (search_vector);
```

2. Apply the migration by connecting to Postgres and executing the SQL.

3. Seed a 'contact' object type for CRM:

```python
import sys, json, uuid
from datetime import datetime, timezone
sys.path.insert(0, 'Code&DBs/Workflow')
from storage.postgres import ensure_postgres_available
conn = ensure_postgres_available()

# Read and execute migration
with open('Code&DBs/Databases/migrations/workflow/017_object_system.sql') as f:
    conn.execute_script(f.read())

# Seed Contact object type
conn.execute(
    """INSERT INTO object_types (type_id, name, description, icon, property_definitions, created_by)
    VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, $5::jsonb, $6)
    ON CONFLICT (type_id) DO UPDATE SET property_definitions = $5::jsonb, description = $3""",
    'contact', 'Contact', 'A person or company you do business with. CRM contact record for tracking relationships, deals, and interactions.',
    'user', json.dumps([
        {'name': 'name', 'type': 'text', 'required': True},
        {'name': 'email', 'type': 'email', 'required': False},
        {'name': 'company', 'type': 'text', 'required': False},
        {'name': 'status', 'type': 'dropdown', 'required': True, 'default': 'lead', 'options': ['lead', 'active', 'churned', 'prospect']},
        {'name': 'phone', 'type': 'text', 'required': False},
        {'name': 'notes', 'type': 'text', 'required': False},
        {'name': 'deal_value', 'type': 'currency', 'required': False},
    ]),
    'system',
)

# Seed 5 sample contacts
contacts = [
    {'name': 'Sarah Chen', 'email': 'sarah@acmecorp.com', 'company': 'Acme Corp', 'status': 'active', 'phone': '555-0101', 'deal_value': 50000, 'notes': 'Key enterprise account, renewing Q3'},
    {'name': 'Marcus Johnson', 'email': 'marcus@techstart.io', 'company': 'TechStart', 'status': 'lead', 'phone': '555-0102', 'deal_value': 15000, 'notes': 'Interested in enterprise plan'},
    {'name': 'Emily Rodriguez', 'email': 'emily@globalfin.com', 'company': 'Global Finance', 'status': 'active', 'phone': '555-0103', 'deal_value': 120000, 'notes': 'Multi-year contract signed'},
    {'name': 'David Kim', 'email': 'david@startupx.co', 'company': 'StartupX', 'status': 'prospect', 'phone': '555-0104', 'deal_value': 8000, 'notes': 'Demo scheduled next week'},
    {'name': 'Lisa Thompson', 'email': 'lisa@megacorp.com', 'company': 'MegaCorp', 'status': 'churned', 'phone': '555-0105', 'deal_value': 75000, 'notes': 'Lost to competitor, follow up in 6 months'},
]

for c in contacts:
    oid = f'obj-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:12]}'
    conn.execute(
        'INSERT INTO objects (object_id, type_id, properties, created_by) VALUES ($1, $2, $3::jsonb, $4) ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING',
        oid, 'contact', json.dumps(c), 'seed',
    )

print(f'Types: {conn.fetchval("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM object_types")}')
print(f'Objects: {conn.fetchval("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM objects")}')
```

Run this Python script to apply migration and seed data. Verify tables exist and data is correct.

jobs.label: add_object_and_workflow_apis
jobs.prompt:
You are working at the Praxis repository root. Use Postgres at $WORKFLOW_DATABASE_URL.

Add API endpoints to Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/api/workflow_api.py for the object system AND for dispatching jobs from the UI.

The file has a do_GET method with inline path matching and a do_POST method. Add these endpoints following the existing patterns.

## GET endpoints (add to do_GET before the 404 fallthrough):

GET /api/object-types
- Query: SELECT type_id, name, description, icon, property_definitions, created_at FROM object_types ORDER BY name
- Return: {types: [...], count: N}

GET /api/objects (requires query param type)
- Parse query string for: type, status (default 'active'), q (optional FTS), limit (default 100)
- Base: SELECT object_id, type_id, properties, status, created_at, updated_at FROM objects WHERE type_id = $1 AND status = $2
- If q: add AND search_vector @@ plainto_tsquery('english', $3)
- Return: {objects: [...], count: N, type_id: ...}

GET /api/objects/:id (path starts with /api/objects/ and has an ID after)
- SELECT * FROM objects WHERE object_id = $1
- Return the object or 404

## POST endpoints (add to do_POST as inline handlers before the _ROUTES dispatch):

POST /api/object-types
- Body: {name, description?, property_definitions, icon?}
- Generate type_id from lowercased name with hyphens + 6-char uuid
- INSERT INTO object_types
- Return created row

POST /api/objects
- Body: {type_id, properties}
- Validate type_id exists
- Generate object_id as 'obj-' + 12-char hex uuid
- INSERT INTO objects
- Return created object

POST /api/objects/update
- Body: {object_id, properties}
- UPDATE objects SET properties = properties || $2::jsonb, updated_at = now() WHERE object_id = $1 RETURNING *
- Return updated object

POST /api/objects/delete
- Body: {object_id}
- UPDATE objects SET status = 'deleted', updated_at = now() WHERE object_id = $1
- Return {deleted: true}

POST /api/dispatch-job
- Body: {prompt, model?}
- Default model: 'anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6'
- Build a temp queue spec JSON file, load via QueueSpec.load(), run via DispatchRunner with pg_conn
- Import: sys.path.insert(0, str(_WORKFLOW_ROOT)); sys.path.insert(0, str(_WORKFLOW_ROOT / 'surfaces' / 'cli'))
- from workflow_runner import QueueSpec, DispatchRunner
- Return: {status, duration_seconds, jobs: [{label, status, stdout (first 4000 chars), stderr (first 2000 chars), duration_seconds}]}

IMPORTANT: All GET handlers use self.subsystems.get_pg_conn(). All POST handlers use self.subsystems.get_pg_conn(). Use json.dumps with default=str for serialization. Add Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * header (already in _send_json).

For the dispatch-job endpoint, write the spec to a tempfile, then clean up:
```python
import tempfile, uuid
job_id = uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]
spec = {'name': f'ui-dispatch-{job_id}', 'queue_id': f'ui-{job_id}', 'phase': 'interactive', 'jobs': [{'label': f'interactive-{job_id}', 'agent': model, 'prompt': prompt}]}
with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(mode='w', suffix='.json', delete=False) as f:
    json.dump(spec, f)
    spec_path = f.name
try:
    loaded = QueueSpec.load(spec_path)
    runner = DispatchRunner(config_root=str(_REPO_ROOT / 'config'), receipts_dir=str(_REPO_ROOT / 'artifacts' / 'dispatch_receipts'), pg_conn=pg)
    result = runner.run_spec(loaded)
finally:
    os.unlink(spec_path)
```

Do NOT break any existing endpoints. Test by running a quick Python verification that the API module loads without errors.

jobs.label: update_modules_for_objects
jobs.prompt:
You are working in Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/.

Update 5 React modules to support the flexible object system, and wire the dispatch form.

## 1. Wire DispatchFormModule (src/modules/tool/DispatchFormModule.tsx)

Replace the handleSubmit that logs to console. Make it:
- POST to /api/dispatch-job with {prompt, model: selectedModel}
- Show loading state during dispatch
- On success: show result.jobs[0].stdout in a scrollable pre block
- On error: show error message
- Make the result area scrollable: maxHeight 300px, overflowY auto, font-family monospace, fontSize 12

## 2. Create DataTableModule (src/modules/display/DataTableModule.tsx)

New module that wraps the DataTable primitive with object system support.
- id: 'data-table', type: 'display', defaultSpan: '2x2'
- Config: { objectType?, endpoint?, path?, columns?, refreshInterval? }
- When objectType is set:
  - Fetch /api/object-types/{objectType} for property_definitions
  - Fetch /api/objects?type={objectType} for rows
  - Auto-derive columns from property_definitions if not in config
  - Map each object: {...obj.properties, object_id: obj.object_id, status: obj.status}
- When endpoint is set: fetch from that endpoint, extract data at path
- Pass columns and rows to the DataTable primitive from src/primitives/DataTable.tsx
- Register in src/modules/display/index.ts

## 3. Update ButtonRowModule (src/modules/input/ButtonRowModule.tsx)

Add createObject support to actions config:
- Action shape: {label, variant?, endpoint?, worldWrite?, createObject?: {typeId, defaults?}}
- When action has createObject: on click, use window.prompt() to ask for the required field (name), then POST to /api/objects with {type_id, properties: {name: value, ...defaults}}
- After creation, write new object_id to World state at 'shared.lastCreatedObject'

## 4. Update SearchPanelModule (src/modules/tool/SearchPanelModule.tsx)

Add objectType config support:
- Config: { endpoint?, placeholder?, objectType? }
- When objectType set: search /api/objects?type={objectType}&q={query}
- Map results: name from first text property, description from remaining properties
- Keep existing registry search as default

## 5. Update KeyValueModule (src/modules/display/KeyValueModule.tsx)

Add object display support:
- Config: { items?, endpoint?, objectType?, objectId? }
- When objectType + objectId set: fetch /api/objects/{objectId}, display properties as key-value pairs
- Label each property using the property name with first letter capitalized

All modules use the existing dark theme CSS variables. Import useModuleData for API fetching. Handle loading and error states.

jobs.label: bind_crm_manifest
jobs.prompt:
You are working at the Praxis repository root. Use Postgres via $WORKFLOW_DATABASE_URL (source scripts/_workflow_env.sh && workflow_load_repo_env at repo root). PATH includes /opt/homebrew/bin.

Update the CRM Dashboard manifest in Postgres to bind its modules to the Contact object type.

Run this Python script:

```python
import sys, json
sys.path.insert(0, 'Code&DBs/Workflow')
from storage.postgres import ensure_postgres_available
conn = ensure_postgres_available()

manifest = {
    'version': 2,
    'grid': '4x4',
    'quadrants': {
        'A1': {'module': 'search-panel', 'span': '2x1', 'config': {'placeholder': 'Search contacts...', 'objectType': 'contact'}},
        'A3': {'module': 'metric', 'config': {'label': 'Active Contacts', 'value': '...', 'color': '#3fb950'}},
        'A4': {'module': 'button-row', 'config': {'actions': [{'label': 'New Contact', 'variant': 'primary', 'createObject': {'typeId': 'contact', 'defaults': {'status': 'lead'}}}]}},
        'B1': {'module': 'data-table', 'span': '4x2', 'config': {'objectType': 'contact', 'columns': [{'key': 'name', 'label': 'Name', 'sortable': True}, {'key': 'email', 'label': 'Email'}, {'key': 'company', 'label': 'Company', 'sortable': True}, {'key': 'status', 'label': 'Status'}, {'key': 'deal_value', 'label': 'Deal Value'}, {'key': 'phone', 'label': 'Phone'}]}},
        'D1': {'module': 'key-value', 'span': '2x1', 'config': {'items': [{'label': 'Total Contacts', 'value': '5'}, {'label': 'Active Deals', 'value': '$268,000'}, {'label': 'Avg Deal Size', 'value': '$53,600'}]}},
        'D3': {'module': 'activity-feed', 'span': '2x1', 'config': {'title': 'Recent CRM Activity'}},
    }
}

conn.execute(
    'UPDATE app_manifests SET manifest = $1::jsonb, updated_at = now() WHERE id = $2',
    json.dumps(manifest), 'crm-dashboard',
)

# Also seed a few more object types for other templates
types = [
    ('task', 'Task', 'A task or to-do item for project management and productivity tracking.',
     [{'name': 'title', 'type': 'text', 'required': True}, {'name': 'status', 'type': 'dropdown', 'options': ['todo', 'in-progress', 'done', 'blocked'], 'default': 'todo'}, {'name': 'assignee', 'type': 'text'}, {'name': 'priority', 'type': 'dropdown', 'options': ['low', 'medium', 'high', 'urgent']}, {'name': 'due_date', 'type': 'date'}, {'name': 'description', 'type': 'text'}]),
    ('bug', 'Bug Report', 'A software bug or defect report for issue tracking.',
     [{'name': 'title', 'type': 'text', 'required': True}, {'name': 'severity', 'type': 'dropdown', 'options': ['P0', 'P1', 'P2', 'P3'], 'default': 'P2'}, {'name': 'status', 'type': 'dropdown', 'options': ['open', 'investigating', 'fixed', 'wont-fix'], 'default': 'open'}, {'name': 'assignee', 'type': 'text'}, {'name': 'description', 'type': 'text'}, {'name': 'steps_to_reproduce', 'type': 'text'}]),
    ('invoice', 'Invoice', 'An invoice for billing and accounts receivable tracking.',
     [{'name': 'invoice_number', 'type': 'text', 'required': True}, {'name': 'client', 'type': 'text', 'required': True}, {'name': 'amount', 'type': 'currency', 'required': True}, {'name': 'status', 'type': 'dropdown', 'options': ['draft', 'sent', 'paid', 'overdue'], 'default': 'draft'}, {'name': 'due_date', 'type': 'date'}, {'name': 'notes', 'type': 'text'}]),
    ('note', 'Note', 'A note or document for knowledge management.',
     [{'name': 'title', 'type': 'text', 'required': True}, {'name': 'content', 'type': 'text'}, {'name': 'category', 'type': 'dropdown', 'options': ['personal', 'work', 'reference', 'archive'], 'default': 'work'}, {'name': 'tags', 'type': 'text'}]),
]

for tid, name, desc, props in types:
    conn.execute(
        'INSERT INTO object_types (type_id, name, description, property_definitions, created_by) VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4::jsonb, $5) ON CONFLICT (type_id) DO UPDATE SET property_definitions = $4::jsonb, description = $3',
        tid, name, desc, json.dumps(props), 'system',
    )
    print(f'  {tid}: {name}')

print(f'\nObject types: {conn.fetchval("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM object_types")}')
print(f'Objects: {conn.fetchval("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM objects")}')
print('CRM manifest updated')
```

Run this script. Verify the CRM manifest is updated and object types are seeded.
